figures.caption: Figure 3: RFI mitigation examples on real data. (a) Scene 3 (snapshot ID: 691840761) and (b)-(e) corresponding results after applying CLEAN, RPCA, RNN-DFT, VFDM respectively. (f) Scene 4 (snapshot ID: 691840910) and (g)-(j) corresponding results after applying CLEAN, RPCA, RNN-DFT, VFDM respectively.

Figure 2: RFI mitigation examples on simulated data. (a) Scene 1 (snapshot ID: 691841314); (b) weak RFI contamination of scene 1 and (c)-(f) corresponding results after applying CLEAN, RPCA, RNN-DFT, VFDM respectively. (g) Scene 2 (snapshot ID: 691834271); (h) hybrid RFI contamination of scene 2 and (i)-(l) corresponding results after applying CLEAN, RPCA, RNNDFT, VFDM respectively.
Table 2: Quantitative evaluation under different RFI modes.

Figure 1: Overview of the Proposed VFDM. The diffusion process starts from RS,0 and Gaussian noise is gradually added to RS,t−1 to obtain RS,t until t = T . The reverse process starts from random noise and generates RS,t−1 from RS,t. The contaminated covariance matrix is incorporated into the reverse process as a condition to guide the reconstruction of the natural covariance matrix.
